new_text: TRv1 treasury program rewrite (2026-02-20): SPEC STATE: TreasuryState { version, authority, total_deposited, total_withdrawn, last_deposit_slot, last_withdrawal_slot }. SPEC INSTRUCTIONS: Initialize, DepositFees, Withdraw, UpdateAuthority, GetBalance. PROGRAM_ID: Treasury11111111111111111111111111111111111. Uses declare_process_instruction! macro, InvokeContext, limited_deserialize, Borsh for state, discriminator byte 0x54. KEY FACTS: workspace Cargo.toml has treasury at programs/treasury; builtins/src/lib.rs registers it; fee_distribution.rs has TODO treasury fee burn; trv1_constants.rs TREASURY_PUBKEY_DEFAULT hardcoded; state.rs discriminator=0x54, SERIALIZED_SIZE=66 bytes. No #[cfg(test)] tests exist. Build: cargo check -p solana-treasury-program --features agave-unstable-api

Session #8 (2026-03-03) Eclipse L2: Answered "missed epoch" question (auto-distribute each epoch vs manual crank). Redesigned early unlock penalty: OLD = stake × rate_bp × 5 / 10000; NEW = rewards_earned_so_far + (original_stake × tier_penalty_bp / 10000). Insight: delegation.stake unchanged by reward distribution, so rewards_earned = lamports - delegation.stake - rent_reserve. Added EARLY_UNLOCK_PENALTY_BP array [0,0,250,500,750,1250] for tiers 0-5. Tier 1 now 0% penalty (free exit). Pending: more penalty refinement expected. Uncommitted: stake_state.rs + projectdawn_config.rs. Tests: stake 127/127, runtime 12/12.

ProjectDawn Feature Gate Implementation (2026-02-09): Added PROJECTDAWN_FEATURE_ID in projectdawn_config.rs (pubkey 0xDA00...0002). Modified apply_feature_activations() to inject ID into FeatureSet.inactive before compute_active_feature_set(). Changed hardcoded projectdawn_enabled: true to false in Bank::default_with_accounts and Bank::new_from_fields. Bank::new_from_parent already inherits. Approach: solana-feature-set external, can't modify; FeatureSet::default() only includes FEATURE_NAMES; inject custom ID into inactive before compute. Activation: create Feature account on-chain with matching pubkey, use 'solana feature activate'. Build: cargo check succeeds.

ARXIV 2506.05109 Metacognitive Learning - Self-Improving Agents: Framework for self-improvement via intrinsic metacognition: (1) Metacognitive Knowledge - self-assess capabilities/tasks/strategies; (2) Metacognitive Planning - determine learning dynamically; (3) Metacognitive Evaluation - reflect on experiences. Key: effective self-improvement needs agent's OWN adaptive evaluation, not fixed human-designed loops. Torus applicability: 17 gates are extrinsic (agent has no say); add meta-gate layer for agent self-assessment. Metacognitive Planning: agent proposes gate tightening/relaxation. Implementable: meta-reflection hook in gate 4 after N sessions with report on false positives/negatives, stored in LanceDB type:metacognition, team lead reviews/approves.
